repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1: finite bridge-rank ATT frontier and binary-proxy non-equivalence witness

published_prior_art.cite: MiaoGengTchetgen2018 identifies proxy effects under rank; MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024 identifies an outcome bridge; ZhangEtAl2025NCDID calibrates NC-DiD; none verified as giving this finite scalar-vs-bridge wedge
RambachanRoth2023, Roth2022Pretest, and KahnLangLang2020 concern pretrend sensitivity or testing, not bridge-branch representability

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 computes the constructive wedge incorrectly: the stated Chebyshev best scalar lambda*=20/7 is not optimal; lambda*=10/3 gives smaller sup residual and omega=0, so the advertised omega != 0 witness is not exhibited.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite witness certificate depends on the same wrong scalar-residual transcript, so the worked focal object W_cal(P) is not independently re-executed successfully.

novelty_flags.label: Related work / Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Gregori2025 is framed as scalar NC-DiD calibration, but the verified EMAC abstract says it builds on proximal causal inference and proposes a semiparametric doubly robust estimator using a pair of negative controls.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The generic open-class nonrepresentation claim only sketches perturbing off-diagonal cells; it does not give a concrete non-corner parametric family whose scalar summaries are fixed while W_cal changes.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Recomputing Exhibit 9.1 gives lambda*=10/3, residuals (1/3,-1/3), rho=1/3, and omega=1.5-(10/3)*0.45=0, contradicting the claimed lambda*=20/7, rho=3/7, omega=3/14.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The P^{PX} primitive table sets U=N but makes Y0 and Y1(0) depend on A within fixed N cells, contradicting the cited negative-control exposure exclusion A not-> Y_t(d) after conditioning on U.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: P^0 is inconsistent across Section 6 and the branch-catalogue exhibit: Section 6 uses uniform 0.25 cells, while the later catalogue uses rows (0.40,0.10) and (0.40,0.10).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the P^{PX} witness with a primitive law satisfying the A-exclusion conditional on U, then recompute B, b, pi, h, rho, omega from raw cells; if omega is meant to be nonzero, choose cells where the true Chebyshev minimizer does not match the bridge correction.
Add an explicit one-parameter or two-parameter open-family witness for Conjecture 2 that holds the scalar diagnostic inputs fixed while changing det(B), rho, or bridge status.

Either reframe Gregori2025 as a proximal negative-control comparator or add a specific scalar-calibration comparator with section/page anchor; the current scalar-vs-bridge contrast leans on an inaccurate comparator bucket.

To reach the flagship floor, upgrade the result from a finite algebraic separation to an iff frontier or strict open-class nonrepresentation theorem tied to at least two named published estimators/workflows, with a computable observable threshold.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version and pivot or rebuild the witness from scratch; the focal-object arithmetic and the claimed negative-control exclusion both fail.
